title: The Governorship of Sa'd b. Abi Waggag in Kufah The Letters of 'Uthman to His Officials and Governors and to the Common People
description: In this year, 'Uthman removed al-Mughirah b. Shu'bah from Kufah and appointed Sa'd b. Abi Wagqag as governor there, according to the account transmitted to me in writing by al-SariShu'ayb-Sayf-al-Mujalid-al-Sha'bi. He says: 'Umar had said, [2802) "I enjoin the Caliph after me to name Sa'd b. Abi Wagqag as governor, for I did not remove him on account of any evil action, and I fear that something of that kind may be attributed to him." The first governor ('amil) whom 'Uthman appointed was Sa'd b. Abi Wagqag in Kufah. He removed al-Mughirah b.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 15
